description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) granted approval to revumenib for the treatment of adult and pediatric patients 1 year and older with relapsed or refractory acute myeloid leukemia and a susceptible .nucleophosmin 1 (NPM1) variant. The approval defines susceptible NPM1 mutations as those that result in a loss of the nucleolar localization signal and the insertion of a new nuclear export signal leading to the accumulation of mutant NPM1 in the cytoplasm of AML cells; the most common of such NPM1 variants in patients with AML being Types A (c.860_863dupTCTG), B (c.863_864insCATG), and D (c.863_864insCCTG).

contributions.description: On 2025-10-24, the US FDA granted approval to revumenib for the treatment of adult and pediatric patients 1 year or older who have no satisfactory alternative treatment options with relapsed or refractory acute myeloid leukemia and a susceptible NPM1 variant.

contributor.name: Van Allen lab
contributor.description: Van Allen lab, Dana-Farber Cancer Institute

indication.indication: REVUFORJ is a menin inhibitor indicated for the treatment of relapsed or refractory acute myeloid leukemia (AML) with a susceptible nucleophosmin 1 (NPM1) mutation in adult and pediatric patients 1 year and older who have no satisfactory alternative treatment options.
indication.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) granted approval to revumenib for the treatment of adult and pediatric patients 1 year and older with relapsed or refractory acute myeloid leukemia and a susceptible .nucleophosmin 1 (NPM1) variant. The approval defines susceptible NPM1 mutations as those that result in a loss of the nucleolar localization signal and the insertion of a new nuclear export signal leading to the accumulation of mutant NPM1 in the cytoplasm of AML cells; the most common of such NPM1 variants in patients with AML being Types A (c.860_863dupTCTG), B (c.863_864insCATG), and D (c.863_864insCCTG).
